parsed_review.summary: CP00-938 is a descriptor-only HRX RiskFixture/AuditFixture bridge across RP30.P05.M04.S01-RP30.P05.M05.S01 (10 units: 9 RiskFixture implementation + 1 AuditFixture security_audit). The pack-level claim is hrx_risk_audit_fixture_bridge_descriptor_verified with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, and that claim is fully supported. Verified: (1) plan, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, HRX_CP938_PACK_BINDING, and prompt distributions all agree (range, unit_count=10, deliverable split 9/1, micro_title split 9/1, next CP00-939/RP30.P05.M05.S02); (2) registry.js builds descriptor-only rows with every runtime/record-write/real-data/score-calculation/runtime-receipt/permission-bypass flag false, and tags the AuditFixture row as a security-audit descriptor; (3) validators.js coverage and descriptor validation enforce each boundary and correctly separate RiskFixture (M04) from AuditFixture (M05); (4) model.test.js has three CP938 tests asserting binding/coverage, descriptor closure, and contract projection with errors==[]; (5) contracts/hrx-people-contract.json carries latest_projection HrxCp938RiskAuditFixtureBridgeDescriptor, validation.valid=true, no_write_attestation all false, and capabilities/safety-gates/artifacts/no-leak-guards that include the CP938 entries; (6) the validate script chains all validators plus the CP897->CP938 binding chain. Authority boundary holds: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), Hermes is used only for deterministic packet/receipt handling (emits_runtime_receipt=false, deterministic_only=true), and enterprise trust is closed/deferred to human approval. No real client/employee/candidate/payroll/audit data, credentials, or secrets are present; no product-state, audit, or permission writes occur. CP178+ hardening checks pass: the auth preflight returned valid JSON (1449 bytes, exit 0), and there are no malformed/0-byte/fenced/tool-call-shaped review evidence artifacts and no prior invalid CP938 attempts (the only invalid-attempt file in the worktree belongs to the unrelated LDIP workstream and is correctly preserved out of scope). One non-blocking P3: command-evidence.json/manifest/construction-inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result are in the expected pre-review pending state (only HRX-local commands recorded exit 0; the full closeout validation ladder and a recorded review receipt remain pending) — this is the normal condition this review gates, not a defect. No P0/P1/P2 findings; nothing blocks this CP closeout goal.

findings.title: Full validation ladder and review receipt pending in closeout artifacts (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only HRX-local checks (node --check x3, node --test on packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate) with exit_code 0 and marks status=pending_final_validation with hermes_gate.status=pending. The broader closeout ladder named in the review baseline (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet evidenced, and manifest.pack_level_claude_review (status=pending_hardened_review, review_receipt_ref=null, verdict=null), construction-inspection.json (verdict=PENDING), adjudication.md (PENDING), and claude-review-result.json (overall_verdict=PENDING) are all still in their pre-review state. This is the expected condition that this read-only review gates, and the artifacts correctly hold production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / status=in_progress, so it is not a defect.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping CP00-938 to closed, run the full validation ladder and confirm each command passes, record exactly one valid hardened read-only review receipt, and update manifest.json, construction-inspection.json, adjudication.md, and claude-review-result.json with the resulting verdict and receipt_ref.
